rulings.Case Title: NOEL A. OBIORA VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD (A CALIFORNIA STATE AGENECY) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Franchise Tax Board'S (A California State Agenecy) Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 26, 2011, line 13, Defendant Franchise Tax Board's (A California State Agency) Motion For Summary Judgment. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF TO SHOW THAT A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT EXISTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A TAX REFUND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MEET THAT BURDEN. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NOEL A. OBIORA VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD (A CALIFORNIA STATE AGENECY) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Noel Obiora'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 26, 2011, line 13, Plaintiff Noel Obiora's Motion For Summary Judgment. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PUT FORTH EVIDENCE THAT, IF TRUE, WOULD ENTITLE HIM TO PREVAIL ON THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A TAX REFUND. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PORFERIO EBORLAS VS. INDYMAC BANK, FSB et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 26, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT ONEWEST BANK, FSB DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant OWB¿s Demurrer to the the 5th (Breach of Covenant), 7th (BP §17200), 8th (Unjust Enrichment), 9th (Quiet Title), 10th (Dec. Relief), 11th (Rescission), and 12th (Inj. Relief) cause of action is overruled. The Court overruled Defendant¿s demurrer as to these cause of action on 4/13/11. Defendant now demurrers based on new arguments which may have some merit but should be raised through a Motion for Reconsideration. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (Civil Conspiracy is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to plead the formation and operation of the conspiracy as to this defendant. Because this is the second attempt Plaintiff has had to allege sufficient facts to support this cause of action and still does not provide anything but conclusory statements as to the alleged conspiracy, the Court does not believe Plaintiff can plead facts to support this cause of action as to this defendant. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY CHEW VS. CHASE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice To Vacate Tentative Ruling On Hearing Held Mar-30-11
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 26, 2011, line 7, PLAINTIFF LARRY CHEW'S Notice To Vacate Tentative Ruling On Hearing Held Mar-30-11. THE COURT SUA SPONTE VACATES THE 3/30/11 AND 4/20/11 RULINGS. THE COURT SUSTAINS DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE HEARING ON 3/30/11 SHOULD NOT HAVE GONE FORWARD BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAD FILED AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE PARTIES PRIOR FILINGS AND PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO FILE A 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE HIS 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 20 DAYS. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MERYL MIKAL et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 26, 2011, line 10, DEFENDANT WILLIAMS M.D., KRISTIN M. SUED ERRONEOUSLY HEREIN, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Dr. Williams¿ Demurrer to the 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff¿s complaint is somewhat conclusory and lumps all of the defendants together. Plaintiff needs to plead with more particularity what each defendant said to constitute the misrepresentation(s). The Plaintiff must also plead reliance with more particularity. Plaintiff must also plead more facts to support its claim for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. =(302/LMG)
